steps.title: Find the growth factor
steps.content:
Let $k=b^{1/n}$. Then $f(x)=ab^{x/n}$ becomes $f(x)=ak^x$. Use the two known values as a ratio:
$$\frac{f(8)}{f(5)}=\frac{189}{7}=27$$
The input increased by $3$, so $k^3=27$ and $k=3$.

steps.title: Use Desmos or multiply from the nearer known value
steps.content:
In Desmos, enter the points $(5,7)$ and $(8,189)$ as lists, then regress $y_1\sim ak^{x_1}$ and evaluate at $x=10$. The mental shortcut is even faster: from $x=8$ to $x=10$ is two unit steps, so multiply by $3^2=9$:
$$f(10)=189\cdot 9=1701$$
The answer is 1701.
